title: Cryptographically Insecure PRNG
description: Using non-cryptographic random number generators for security-sensitive values (tokens, session IDs, nonces, salts) produces predictable output (OWASP A02:2021 / CWE-338).

finding.message: Insecure PRNG '{{token}}' in {{file}} at line {{line}} — use a CSPRNG for security contexts.

references: Python: use secrets.token_bytes(), secrets.token_hex(), or os.urandom().
Node.js: use crypto.randomBytes() from the built-in crypto module.
Java: use java.security.SecureRandom; never use java.util.Random for security.
